title: Chyme sampling from the digestive tract of Octopus maya
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"75nt7","text":"This protocol details the chyme sampling from the digestive tract of Octopus maya.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":69,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"a1fcn","text":"Materials and equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6sbim","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4lckf","text":"Surgical tools: scissors and clamps","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1m02l","text":"Centrifuge capable of reaching  ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":31,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"299ju","text":"Tissue homogenizer (e.g., ULTRA-TURRAX IKA T18 basic)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7sg9o","text":"Liquid nitrogen","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"967sd","text":"Cryogenic storage containers (  freezer)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":30,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"c8s3u","text":"Eppendorf tubes (1.5 mL or 2 mL)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"db3mt","text":"Protective equipment: gloves and lab coat","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"centrifuge","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"centrifuge":"13370","duration":0,"error_centrifuge":false,"error_temperature":false,"guid":"2C0FF4A0D72F11EF88550A58A9FEAC02","id":0,"label":"","pause":null,"preset":[],"scientificNotation":{"centrifuge":false,"temperature":false},"start":null,"temperature":"","temperatureUnit":10,"unit":34}},"1":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"2C0FF580D72F11EF88550A58A9FEAC02","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"-40","unit":10}}}}
steps: Ensure all tools and equipment are clean and sterilized before starting.
Prepare the workspace with necessary materials, ensuring easy access to surgical instruments, centrifuge, liquid nitrogen, and Eppendorf tubes.
Use proper personal protective equipment (PPE) throughout the procedure.
Carefully dissect the digestive tract of Octopus maya, ensuring the integrity of each section.
Identify and separate the following sections: Anterior salivary glands (BM) Crop (Cr) Stomach (St) Caecum (Ca) Digestive Gland (DG)
Use surgical clamps to isolate and prevent mixing of digestive contents between sections.
Open each section carefully to access the chyme.
Transfer the chyme into labeled Eppendorf tubes.
Centrifuge the chyme samples at 13370 x g, °C, 5 minutes to separate solids from the supernatant.
Collect the supernatant and immediately freeze it in liquid nitrogen.
Store the frozen samples at -40 °C until analysis.
Liquefy and homogenize the glands using a tissue homogenizer (e.g., ULTRA-TURRAX IKA T18 basic) at an appropriate speed for uniform homogenization.
Transfer the homogenized sample into an Eppendorf tube.
Centrifuge the sample at 13370 x g, °C, 5 minutes to obtain a clear supernatant.
Freeze the supernatant in liquid nitrogen immediately after centrifugation.

Store the sample at -40 °C until further analysis. Notes and precautions: Ensure the surgical clamps are properly placed to prevent cross-contamination of digestive contents. Work quickly when handling biological samples to preserve enzyme activity. Properly label all tubes with the section of the digestive tract and the sample type for accurate identification. Use liquid nitrogen with caution and follow appropriate safety protocols to avoid burns or injuries. Storage: Samples should remain frozen at -40 °C to preserve enzyme and metabolite integrity until the time of analysis.
creator: Daisy Pineda-Suazo
